items.title: Exercising the team option for Micah Potter was an easy call for the Pacers — and mostly a formality.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Exercising the team option for Micah Potter was an easy call for the Pacers — and mostly a formality.

His contract is non-guaranteed, preserving flexibility. It doesn’t become guaranteed until Jan. 10, giving the FO time (and an option) to solidify its backup center plan.

items.title: The Toronto Raptors are exercising the $2.41 million team option on Trayce Jackson-Davis, league sources told HoopsHype. The 26-year-old forward has averaged 6.2 points on 63.1% shooting from the field and 4.4 rebounds in 14.2 minutes in 3 seasons with the Warriors and Raptors.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: The Toronto Raptors are exercising the $2.41 million team option on Trayce Jackson-Davis, league sources told HoopsHype. The 26-year-old forward has averaged 6.2 points on 63.1% shooting from the field and 4.4 rebounds in 14.2 minutes in 3 seasons with the Warriors and Raptors.

items.title: 2026 NBA Free Agency: Live Analysis
items.author: Last Night In Basketball
items.body_excerpt: Here’s how I’m once again handling free agency this year. I’ll use this space to provide quick thoughts on most (if not necessarily all) of the signings that get reported this week, as they come. This post will be free and unlocked for all to read. Before we get started, keep in…

items.title: It appears the Sixers will not work out a long-term deal with Dominick Barlow and instead are set to pick up his $3,415,000 team option today:
items.author: Adam Aaronson
items.body_excerpt:
It appears the Sixers will not work out a long-term deal with Dominick Barlow and instead are set to pick up his $3,415,000 team option today:

www.phillyvoice.com/sixers-news-...

media.title: Report: Sixers accept Dominick Barlow's $3.4 million team option
media.description: Dominick Barlow averaged 7.7 points, 4.8 rebounds, 1.2 assists, 0.9 blocks, 0.7 steals and 23.8 minutes across 71 games (59 starts) for the 2025-26 Sixers.

items.title: [Men’s Health] Ben Simmons: “Maybe I’ll go back to Philly. Miami would be nice. And not because it’s Miami—I like Erik Spoelstra, I like the Heat, I like their organization, I like the culture.”
items.author: YujiDomainExpansion
